data.title: Bonfida to Hold Hackathon on May 14th
data.description:
Bonfida will co-host the SNS Identity Track at the Colosseum Frontier Hackathon, with Demo Day scheduled for May 14th. The track offers $5,000 in prizes across two themes and is co-hosted with Superteam Malaysia and MagicBlock.

Refer to the official tweet by FIDA:

sns.sol
@sns

Two weeks left for @colosseum Frontier Hackathon.

Building on identity infra? Then SNS Identity Track at @ns is for you: $5,000 in prizes, two themes, Demo Day on May 14.

Co-hosted with @SuperteamMY and @magicblock. pic.twitter.com/u8qYBDWJxr

Apr 29, 2026

FIDA Info

Bonfida is a group of experts in cryptocurrencies, analytics and engineering. Their expertise and focus are largely based on growing blockchain technology by enhancing the world of decentralized identities. Through their accumulated experience they have come to realize that a core component of identity whether on or off-chain is your name. This is how work on the Solana Name Service (SNS) began.

SNS has a simple mission of providing a decentralized and yet affordable way to map domain names (represented as .sol) to on-chain data. Where on-chain data can be anything from a Solana address to IPFS CID, images, text, and more.

Fuller, Smith & Turner PLC (FSTA)

Fuller, Smith & Turner PLC: Transaction in own shares

29-Apr-2026 / 18:27 GMT/BST

Fuller, Smith & Turner P.L.C. (“the Company” or “Fuller’s”)

Transaction in own shares

The Company announces that it has purchased the following number of its “A” Ordinary Shares of 40p each on the London Stock Exchange through Deutsche Bank AG, London Branch (trading for these purposes as Deutsche Numis) ("Deutsche Numis") as part of its share buyback programme announced on 21 January 2026 (the “Programme”).

633.2612

The Company intends to hold the repurchased shares in Treasury.

Following the purchase of the said shares, Fuller’s listed issued share capital consists of 36,446,686 “A” Ordinary Shares of 40p each. Of this total 5,163,233 “A” Ordinary Shares are held in Treasury. Therefore, the total number of listed voting rights in the Company for the purpose of Disclosure and Transparency Rule 5.6.1 (calculated in accordance with Disclosure and Transparency Rule 5.6.2) is 31,283,453. This number may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in Fuller’s, under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) no 596/2014 (as incorporated into UK domestic law by the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018) (the Market Abuse Regulation), the schedule below contains detailed information about the purchases made.

data.title: VeChain to Hold Live Stream on X on April 30th
data.description:
VeChain will hold an X Space with its leadership team on April 30th. The session will cover recent updates, the upcoming agentic ecosystem, the B3MO Quest launch, and VeChain's view on AI and VET.

Refer to the official tweet by VET:

VeChain
@vechainofficial

Tomorrow, join a high octane X space with the VeChain leadership team, as we dive into recent updates including the upcoming agentic ecosystem, the B3MO Quest launch, and our vision on the future of AI + $VET.

Bookmark & be there! 👇https://t.co/e8KEYd1cqR pic.twitter.com/wA5Rhccnv2

Apr 29, 2026

VET Info

VeChain (VET) is a blockchain platform specifically designed to enhance transparency in supply chains.

VeChain's technology is built upon two tokens: VET and VTHO. The VET token primarily serves as a means of payment or money transfer among VeChain users. On the other hand, the VTHO token is utilized to pay for transaction fees and interact with smart contracts on the VeChain blockchain.

It is important to note that VeChain has established collaborations with numerous prominent companies and organizations. For instance, the project has partnered with international consulting firm PwC, automobile manufacturer BMW, and others. This is attributed to VeChain's blockchain approach, which is highly suitable for industrial applications, particularly in the realm of supply chain management.

data.title: Robinhood Stock Falls After Earnings Fall Short. Blame the Crypto Slump. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Robinhood Markets reported first-quarter earnings that missed Wall Street's expectations because of a drop in cryptocurrency trading revenue and moderating investor trading activity.

The brokerage firm posted earnings per share of 38 cents on revenue of $1.07 billion. Analysts were looking for 39 cents and $1.14 billion, according to estimates compiled by FactSet. For the same period a year ago, earnings per share were 37 cents on revenue of $927 million.

Shares of Robinhood dropped 9.8% in premarket trading Wednesday.

A monthslong slump in prices for Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies has weighed on Robinhood's stock, which is down 27% this year. The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF is down 15%.

Under CEO Vlad Tenev, Robinhood has been striving to expand its sources of revenue and become a one-stop shop for customers' every financial need. It has launched credit cards, financial advice, and banking services for clients. Robinhood Strategies, the company's robo-advisor, reached more than 285,000 funded customers and more than $1.6 billion in assets under management at the end of the quarter.

Robinhood is also winning more customers for its Gold subscription service — the company said the number of subscribers hit a record 4.3 million, up 36% year over year. Robinhood Gold subscription revenue for the first quarter rose 32% to $50 million.

"Product velocity continues to accelerate," CFO Shiv Verma said during the company's earnings call. "We are investing for the long term and aggressively utilizing AI across the business."

But the majority of Robinhood's revenue is transaction-based, and a slowdown in customer's trading activity can be a hindrance for the brokerage firm. Transaction-based revenue was $623 million for the first quarter, up 6% from the same period a year ago, but down 20% from the fourth quarter of 2025.

The company said cryptocurrencies revenue fell 47% year over year to $134 million.

Robinhood has also leaned into sports betting via its prediction markets offering, which enables customers to wager on the outcome of events such as football and basketball games. The company said other transaction revenue, which primarily consists of event contracts revenue, soared 320% to $147 million. The first quarter included the Super Bowl.

Tenev is optimistic that prediction markets will become a more integral part of the investing landscape. "I think the asset class will continue to grow and we are beginning to see diversification outside of sports," he said during the company's earnings call late Tuesday.

It may also become a more lucrative business line for Robinhood. The brokerage firm currently relies on prediction market exchange Kalshi to help power its offering for customers, but Robinhood has unveiled plans to work with Susquehanna International Group develop to develop its own prediction markets exchange. This will allow Robinhood to better control the economics of its prediction markets offering as well as the user experience, according to executives. Tenev expects there will be some consolidation among prediction markets providers.

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Mondelez's 1Q Results Build Confidence for 2027 — Market Talk
data.description: Mondelez's 1Q results are cause for increased confidence about the company's prospects next year, Stifel analysts say in a note. Revenue growth and profit performance were strong, and higher cocoa costs seem to be gradually abating, but the real bright spots are sequential improvements in volume across each major category and region, as well as overall organic sales growth of +3%, the analysts say. Looking ahead to 2027 the analysts say, "we believe the company is well positioned to outperform its high-single-digit earnings growth target while increasing investment to maintain sustainable topline momentum." Mondelez rises 5.4%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Stanley Black & Decker 10-Q: $3.85B Revenue, $0.39 GAAP EPS
data.description:
Stanley Black & Decker reported quarterly net sales of $3,846.4 million, up 3% year-over-year, with GAAP diluted earnings per share of $0.39 and non‑GAAP diluted EPS of $0.80 after adjustments. Revenue growth was driven by pricing and foreign exchange benefits, while volume declined modestly. The company reported GAAP net earnings of $59.6 million and non‑GAAP net earnings of $122.2 million after pre-tax adjustments totaling $81.0 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $3,846.4 million, +3% YoY (price +3%, FX +3%, volume -3%).
  • Gross Profit: $1,157.3 million; gross margin 30.1% of sales (adjusted 30.2%).
  • Earnings before income taxes: $84.8 million (impacted by $81.0 million pre-tax non‑GAAP adjustments).
  • Net Earnings (GAAP): $59.6 million.
  • Net Earnings (Non‑GAAP): $122.2 million after adjustments.
  • Diluted EPS (GAAP): $0.39 per share.
  • Diluted EPS (Non‑GAAP): $0.80 per share (adjusted).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and segment performance: Net sales growth was led by Engineered Fastening (+10%) while Tools & Outdoor increased 2%.
  • Channel and geographic trends: Tools & Outdoor experienced lower North America volume but stronger international sell‑in and professional conversions.
  • Brand momentum: Pricing actions and professional conversions supported core brands including DEWALT and CRAFTSMAN.
  • Portfolio and operational actions: Completed the CAM divestiture and executed supply‑chain footprint actions and restructuring aimed at reducing costs.
  • Cost and productivity programs: Completed a Global Cost Reduction program with approximately $2.1 billion run‑rate savings and is pursuing further footprint rationalization.

Original SEC Filing: STANLEY BLACK & DECKER, INC. [ SWK ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Constellium Q1: Revenue $2,461M, EPS $1.42 — Strong YoY Profit Recovery
data.description:
Constellium reported first-quarter revenue of $2,461 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.42, driven by higher revenue per ton amid slightly lower shipments. The company delivered a marked improvement in profitability with net income rising to $196 million from $38 million a year earlier. Management cited metal price effects on revenue and positive operating cash flow despite working capital impacts from higher inventories and receivables.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2,461 million, up 24% year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $337 million (as implied by disclosed figures).
  • Operating Income: $254 million (as implied by disclosed figures).
  • Net Income: $196 million, improved from $38 million in the prior year.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $1.42 attributable to equity holders of Constellium.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Mix: Packaging & Automotive Rolled Products (P&ARP) represented 60% of revenue and 58% of Adjusted EBITDA, reflecting robust demand in packaging and automotive rolled products.
  • Segment Momentum: Aerospace & Transportation shipments rose 18% year-over-year, supported by demand in aerospace, transportation and defense and constrained North American supply.
  • Operations & Capacity: Continued investments in recycling and casting capabilities; the company operates 24 manufacturing sites and 3 R&D centers with approximately 11,500 employees.
  • Working Capital & Cash Flow: Higher metal prices elevated inventories and receivables, while operations still generated positive operating cash flow for the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: CONSTELLIUM SE [ CSTM ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: SOL price drops towards $83 as Solana ETF flows dwindle
data.description:
Solana’s native token, Solana (SOL), traded near $83 on Wednesday after failing to hold above the $85 mark, extending its decline as investor momentum cooled and exchange-traded fund (ETF) inflows slowed.

At the time of writing, SOL was trading at around $83.40, posting a modest 0.4% gain over the past 24 hours.

Despite the slight daily increase, the broader trend remains under pressure.

The token has fallen 5.7% over the past seven days and remains down more than 43% over the previous year.

Weak ETF demand adds pressure to price action

Solana’s recent decline has coincided with fading institutional enthusiasm tied to spot crypto investment products.

Earlier optimism surrounding Solana-focused ETF products had supported stronger buying activity, helping SOL maintain higher trading ranges.

However, recent data suggests those inflows have moderated significantly, removing an important source of upward pressure.

Institutional participation remains a key factor for major digital assets in 2026, particularly as firms such as BlackRock, Fidelity, and Grayscale continue to shape broader crypto market sentiment through regulated investment products.

While Solana remains a prominent altcoin in institutional discussions, weaker ETF-related momentum has reduced buying urgency in recent sessions.

This shift has left SOL more vulnerable to technical breakdowns, especially as broader crypto markets remain cautious amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

Technical indicators point to growing downside risk

SOL’s rejection below $88 has emerged as a key technical development.

Price charts now show repeated lower highs—a structure often associated with increasing seller control.

Momentum indicators such as the Relative Strength Index (RSI) have also softened, pointing to weakening buyer strength.

Immediate support is forming near $80, a level traders are closely monitoring.

A break below this threshold could expose SOL to further downside toward the $75–$70 range.

Some projections also identify $63–$67 as a stronger medium-term support zone if bearish pressure intensifies.

Trading volume remains elevated

Despite weakening momentum, trading activity in Solana remains substantial.

SOL recorded more than $3.74 billion in 24-hour trading volume, reflecting active participation from both short-term traders and longer-term investors adjusting positions.

High trading volume during declining price action often indicates increased defensive positioning, with market participants reacting to uncertain signals rather than strong conviction buying.

This suggests traders are closely watching near-term support levels while waiting for clearer catalysts.

Long-term fundamentals remain intact

Although technical indicators currently favour caution, Solana’s broader ecosystem remains relevant.

The blockchain continues to rank among the largest smart contract platforms, with ongoing adoption across decentralised finance, NFTs, and payment infrastructure.

Companies such as Visa have previously integrated Solana for stablecoin settlement initiatives, while developers continue building on its high-speed network.

SOL also remains significantly above its historical low of $0.5008 recorded in May 2020, though it is still roughly 71.5% below its all-time high of $293.31 reached in January 2025.

This contrast highlights the gap between Solana’s long-term growth trajectory and its current technical correction.
